explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in D tier with an overall score of 19 from a base nutrition score of 25. Context dragged it down by 6 points.

topPros.title: adds hydrating volume
topPros.explanation: It can bulk out meals with almost no calories.

topPros.title: easy salad base
topPros.explanation: Very practical in sandwiches, burgers, and salads.

topPros.title: mild and easy to tolerate
topPros.explanation: A practical plus for people who dislike bitter greens.

topCons.title: micronutrient payoff is weak
topCons.explanation: That is a major problem in a category meant to reward nutrient density.

topCons.title: fibre payoff is weak for a vegetable
topCons.explanation: It does not offer much of the maintenance-and-repair value the category is supposed to reward.

topCons.title: mostly water with limited nutritional return
topCons.explanation: Very low payoff compared with stronger vegetables.

narrationNotes: Context weakened the final tier case by 6 points.

closing.summary: Iceberg Lettuce does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.

scenes.narrationText: 0.1g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, the question is whether the payoff is worth the plate space.

scenes.narrationText: 3g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
